calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-25-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Approving Paga Settlement
Off Calendar Mar-11-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kathleen, M. Pratt, esq. (714-908-4247) of Workplace Legal for Plaintiff.
No other appearances.

Not argued, Counsel presented proposed order. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF KRISTY HARRIS' Motion For An Order Approving PAGA Settlement.

Plaintiff Kristy Harris's motion for an order approving PAGA settlement is granted. No opposition filed, and the settlement meets the standards for approval under Lab. Code 2699. In particular, the parties have not agreed to release or discharge any PAGA claims by any other individual, whether brought individually or on behalf of other aggrieved employees. The Court finds that the allocation of $15,000 in civil penalties to the settlement of plaintiff's PAGA claims, in relation to the settlement amount allocated to plaintiff's individual claims in the parties' confidential settlement agreement (which the Court has reviewed in camera), is reasonable in light of plaintiff's statement of damages and the maximum civil penalties, wages, damages, and attorneys' fees that could be awarded on those respective claims, defendants' potential defenses to the PAGA claims, and the concerns regarding the collectability of a judgment against defendants, among other factors. The Court finds that the proposed agreement is fair and adequate in view of the purposes and policies of the statute. (See Flores v. Starwood Hotels & Resorts Worldwide, Inc. (C.D. Cal. 2017) 253 F.Supp.3d 1074, 1077, citing O'Connor v. Uber Techs., Inc. (N.D. Cal. 2016) 201 F.Supp.3d 1110, 1132-35.)

Order is signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-28-2019 Continued To Apr-10-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Plaintiff Kristy Harris' Motion For An Order Approving Settlement Of Private Attorneys General Act Claims
Off Calendar Jan-03-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kathleen Pratt, Esq. 714-478-2352 for Plaintiff through Court Call.
John McIntyre, Jr. Esq. 408-298-6611 of Shea & McIntyre for Defendant Paul Armstrong through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court asks questions re: filing and proof of service, release as to aggrieved parties. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Plaintiff Kristy Harris' Motion For An Order Approving Settlement Of Private Attorneys General Act Claims.

Denied without Prejudice to being refiled on a better showing. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
